skill.name: e2e-testing-patterns
skill.description: Guidance for designing, implementing, and debugging Playwright and Cypress end-to-end test suites.
skill.summary: Build reliable Playwright and Cypress E2E tests for critical user workflows.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, network, environment access, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found them inside Markdown documentation and TypeScript test examples. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or executable skill script was found, so the residual risk is low and publication is acceptable with normal review.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported backtick and command patterns are Markdown fences, inline code, TypeScript examples, and documented npx debugging commands rather than code executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are visibly inside Markdown code blocks or comments. No executable script file or runtime hook is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Network Patterns Are Local Test Configuration
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The network findings show localhost base URLs, route interception, response waiting, and Cypress request examples used to teach E2E testing patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs and request calls are limited to example test flows and local application routes. I found no external collection endpoint or data exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Is CI And Test Credential Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. process.env.CI controls Playwright retries and workers, and process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD is a placeholder for test credentials in fixture guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable references are embedded in configuration snippets. They do not read or transmit secrets from an executable skill component.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Signals Are Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak crypto and reconnaissance detections appear to match ordinary testing words, browser device names, shard examples, selector guidance, and debugging text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose or framework configuration, not cryptographic operations or host reconnaissance commands. No evidence of malicious semantic intent was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Example Shell Commands In Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes documented commands such as npx Playwright debugging invocations. These are user-facing examples and are not automatically executed by the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are inside a fenced Markdown section under debugging guidance. They require explicit user action to run.

dangerous_patterns.title: Example Environment Variable Reads
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows process.env usage for CI configuration and an admin password placeholder. This is acceptable documentation but should be copied only into trusted test projects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples demonstrate standard Playwright configuration and fixtures. No surrounding code sends these values elsewhere.

dangerous_patterns.title: Example Request Interception And API Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill demonstrates Playwright and Cypress request interception, including a role modification example for tests. This is normal E2E testing guidance, not hidden network behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network operations are framework examples scoped to application routes. They are visible documentation, not concealed runtime behavior.

content.value_statement: End-to-end tests often become slow, flaky, and hard to maintain. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical Playwright and Cypress patterns for stable user workflow coverage.
actual_capabilities: Explains when E2E tests are appropriate and when lower-level tests are better.
Provides Playwright configuration patterns for browsers, retries, traces, screenshots, and CI behavior.
Shows page object and fixture patterns for maintainable test data and login flows.
Demonstrates reliable waiting, assertions, network interception, and request mocking.
Covers Cypress configuration, custom commands, intercepts, and stable selector strategy.
Includes advanced guidance for visual regression, sharding, accessibility scans, and debugging.
limitations: It is a guidance-only Markdown skill and does not include runnable helper scripts in the package.
Examples need adaptation to each application, authentication model, and CI environment.
It does not replace product-specific test design or manual risk assessment.
Referenced resources and scripts are listed in the text but are not present in this scanned package.

use_cases.title: Start A New E2E Suite
use_cases.description: Create a focused Playwright or Cypress baseline for login, checkout, signup, and other critical user journeys.

use_cases.title: Reduce Test Flakiness
use_cases.description: Replace fixed waits, brittle selectors, and coupled test data with deterministic waits, stable selectors, and isolated fixtures.

use_cases.title: Prepare CI Browser Testing
use_cases.description: Plan retries, browser projects, screenshots, traces, sharding, and failure artifacts for a deployment pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Choose E2E Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new test suite.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the e2e-testing-patterns skill to identify which workflows in my app should be covered by E2E tests. Separate critical paths from cases better covered by unit or integration tests.

prompt_templates.title: Create Playwright Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the e2e-testing-patterns skill to design a Playwright folder structure with configuration, page objects, fixtures, and test data cleanup for my application.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Flaky Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the e2e-testing-patterns skill to review this flaky E2E test strategy. Recommend better waits, selectors, assertions, network handling, and failure artifacts.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize CI Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the e2e-testing-patterns skill to propose an advanced CI plan for browser projects, retries, sharding, visual regression, accessibility scans, and test reporting.

output_examples.input: I need E2E tests for a signup flow.
output_examples.output: A concise test plan covering account creation, validation errors, successful redirect, email verification boundaries, fixture data, selectors, and cleanup steps.

output_examples.input: My Cypress tests fail randomly in CI.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis checklist that replaces fixed delays with assertions, stabilizes selectors, isolates test data, captures screenshots, and mocks slow external services.

output_examples.input: We want Playwright in a multi-browser pipeline.
output_examples.output: A CI-oriented recommendation for Chromium, Firefox, WebKit, mobile coverage, retry policy, traces, screenshots, and shard planning.

best_practices: Focus E2E tests on critical user behavior instead of implementation details.
Use stable selectors, deterministic waits, isolated data, and meaningful user-visible assertions.
Capture traces, screenshots, videos, and reports only where they help diagnose failures.
anti_patterns: Do not use fixed sleep timers as the main synchronization strategy.
Do not cover every edge case with E2E tests when unit or integration tests are faster.
Do not couple tests through shared state, execution order, or unmanaged test data.

faq.question: Does this skill run Playwright or Cypress tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and examples. Users still need to install tools and run tests in their own project.

faq.question: Can it help choose between Playwright and Cypress?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare patterns from both tools and suggest a fit based on browser coverage, CI needs, and team workflow.

faq.question: Does it include ready-made test files?
faq.answer: No. It includes example snippets and structure guidance that must be adapted to the target application.

faq.question: Can it help with flaky tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes deterministic waits, stable selectors, isolated data, and useful failure artifacts.

faq.question: Does it cover accessibility testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Playwright accessibility pattern using axe-core and scoped scans.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed risk patterns are documentation examples, and no malicious behavior or prompt injection was found.
